meta.subtitle: Roadmap
meta.currentState: Roadmap dashboard built; requirements freeze in progress; no production agents implemented yet.
meta.nextFocus: Freeze the Manager Agent task lifecycle and approval matrix before building the first runnable agent service.

stats.label: Overall
stats.detail: Real progress based on completed project work: dashboard scaffold exists, but backend, agents, database, scheduler, and integrations are not built yet.

stats.label: Agents
stats.detail: Eight specialist agents are mapped as requirements, not implemented services.

stats.label: Ready
stats.detail: Only the roadmap dashboard is ready for review. Agent implementation is still pending.

stats.label: Decision Items
stats.detail: These decisions should be frozen before core development begins.

stats.label: Focus
stats.detail: The Manager Agent is the control layer for routing, approvals, and schedule ownership.

agents.name: Agency Manager
agents.purpose: Central dispatcher for requests, routing, approvals, schedules, and operational visibility.
risks: Too much autonomy before approvals are tested
agents.statusAction: Finalize task lifecycle, approval matrix, and run log schema.

agents.name: Development
agents.purpose: Builds web/app work, writes tests, opens pull requests, and prepares deployments.
agents.compact: Code, review, test, PR, deploy handoff.
risks: Client repo credentials must stay isolated
agents.statusAction: Wait until core task and approval system is frozen, then design the GitHub workflow.

agents.name: Design
agents.purpose: Produces UI directions, brand systems, campaign visuals, and handoff-ready creative briefs.
agents.statusAction: Freeze the client brand kit schema after the Manager Agent requirements are stable.

agents.name: Video Production
agents.purpose: Plans short-form and campaign video scripts, storyboards, captions, and edit briefs.
agents.statusAction: Keep planned until creative asset and approval structures are defined.

agents.name: Google Market
agents.purpose: Handles SEO, Google Ads planning, keyword strategy, landing-page checks, and performance reports.
risks: Ad spend changes need strict permission
Analytics data can be noisy or delayed
agents.statusAction: Start with read-only reporting before any ad budget action is allowed.

agents.name: Social Market
agents.purpose: Creates platform-specific calendars, posts, hooks, campaigns, and approval-ready drafts.
agents.statusAction: Prepare as the first specialist after Manager because it gives fast agency value.

agents.name: Sales
agents.purpose: Researches leads, drafts outreach, prepares proposals, and keeps pipeline records current.
risks: Lead data must be sourced responsibly
agents.statusAction: Choose the first CRM and map required pipeline fields.

agents.name: QA and Governance
agents.purpose: Reviews output quality, factual accuracy, brand fit, permissions, and publish readiness.
risks: Weak governance makes every other agent unsafe
Review rules can become too vague
agents.statusAction: Create the first cross-agent QA checklist before any publishing tools are connected.

phases.title: Requirement Freeze
phases.summary: Turn this dashboard into the shared source for roadmap, requirements, tasks, and decisions.
phases.todo: Freeze MVP agents, statuses, approval matrix, and client knowledge shape.
phases.statusAction: Complete the requirements dashboard and decide the first agent build order.

phases.title: Independent Core
phases.summary: Build owned backend, database, scheduler, queue, and file/memory foundations.
phases.todo: Choose FastAPI vs Node and define database schema.

phases.title: Agent MVP
phases.summary: Ship Manager, Social Market, and Sales agents with tool calls and approval gates.
phases.todo: Create first runnable Agents SDK service.
phases.statusAction: Start after the independent core exists.

phases.title: Delivery Agents
phases.summary: Add Development, Design, Video, and Google Market workflows after the control layer works.
phases.todo: Define safe tool permissions per specialist.
phases.statusAction: Start after MVP agents are reliable.

phases.title: Agency OS
phases.summary: Unify dashboards, reports, costs, schedules, evals, and role-based access.
phases.statusAction: Start after core workflows are proven.

stack.name: React + Tailwind
stack.next: Use this dashboard as the requirements surface.

stack.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
stack.next: Add when building the first Manager Agent prototype.

stack.name: Redis or open-source queue
stack.next: Add for scheduled and background agent jobs.

stack.name: Docker
stack.next: Use for local database and service orchestration.

stack.name: GitHub + Codex
stack.next: Commit the roadmap and begin branch-based changes.

decisions.label: CRM choice
decisions.detail: Pending. Choose the first CRM target before Sales Agent implementation. Recommended low-dependency path: start with a simple internal pipeline table, then connect HubSpot, Zoho, or Pipedrive only if needed.

decisions.label: MVP agent order
decisions.detail: Suggested order: Agency Manager first, then Social Market and Sales. This gives routing, approvals, content planning, and lead workflow before deeper integrations.

decisions.label: Approval roles
decisions.detail: Pending. Define who can approve client email, publishing, budget changes, deployment, and data edits. No agent should perform those actions without this matrix.

decisions.label: Client file schema
decisions.detail: Pending. Define the required client knowledge folders: brand, offers, services, assets, reports, meetings, approvals, and credentials references.

decisions.label: Backend language
decisions.detail: Suggested: Python FastAPI for the agent backend because the OpenAI Agents SDK workflow is strong there, while keeping the dashboard in React.

decisions.label: Weekly reports
decisions.detail: Pending. Decide the first recurring report format: client summary, active tasks, wins, blockers, next-week plan, and metrics snapshot.
